duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setLockDuration validates new duration using block.timestamp but assigns unlockTime using lastLockTime, allowing users to set unlockTime to a past timestamp and immediately withdraw locked tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same math flaw in setLockDuration where validation uses block.timestamp but assignment uses lastLockTime. ECON-H001 selected for its comprehensive attack economics, victim impact analysis, and clear exploit scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: lockOnBehalf allows any caller to lock 0 tokens for a victim, unconditionally resetting their unlockTime and permanently freezing their funds at near-zero cost
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same griefing vector: _lock unconditionally overwrites unlockTime regardless of _quantity, and lockOnBehalf permits any caller with zero quantity. ECON-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating with most comprehensive economics and victim impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: USD price governance vote exclusivity violated: approveUSDPrice does not check disapprovals mapping, allowing an operator to both disapprove and approve, inflating counts and potentially deadlocking proposals
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 9 threats describe the same missing cross-check between approvals and disapprovals mappings. TECH-H016 and OPS2-H013 specifically highlight the deadlock angle but share the same root cause. ECON-H005 selected for HIGH rating with comprehensive coverage of both vote inflation and stuck-proposal scenarios, plus economic impact. Note: TECH2-H005 here refers to the 'Multisig Governance Bypass via Disapprove-then-Approve Vote Inflation' entry.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer token accounting insolvency: _lock records lockedToken.quantity += _quantity without measuring actual tokens received, leading to cumulative deficit and last-withdrawer loss
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same missing balance-delta check in _lock for fee-on-transfer tokens. ECON-H006 selected for HIGH rating with most detailed economics including early/late withdrawer dynamics. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the 'Protocol Insolvency with Fee-on-Transfer Tokens' entry (not the uint32 truncation entry).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Phantom remainder persists after full unlock: unlock() decrements quantity but never clears remainder, enabling discounted NFT minting in future lockdrops using tokens no longer locked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same bug: unlock only modifies quantity, leaving remainder intact for exploitation in subsequent lockdrops. ECON2-H008 selected for HIGH rating with most detailed attack economics and clearest description of the invariant violation (remainder > locked quantity).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Locking outside active lockdrop period unconditionally zeroes stored remainder: local variable defaults to 0 and overwrites storage when NFT calculation block is skipped
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 8 threats describe the same unconditional overwrite of lockedToken.remainder with an uninitialized (0) local variable when lockdrop is inactive. TECH-H006 selected for most precise code references (line numbers for initialization, conditional block, and unconditional assignment). Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to the 'Loss of NFT Remainder Value when Locking Outside Lockdrop Period' entry (not the rebasing entry).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: lockOnBehalf can overwrite recipient's playerSettings.lockDuration when it is 0, forcing a duration preference without consent
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats describe the same sovereignty violation: _lock's fallback sets _lockDuration from lockdrop.minLockDuration when recipient's duration is 0, then unconditionally writes to playerSettings. TECH-H007 selected for clearest description. Note: TECH2-H008 here is 'Unauthorized Modification of Victim's Global Lock Duration' and TECH2-H006 is 'Non-consensual lock duration override' (the second entries with those IDs).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing SafeERC20 wrappers: raw IERC20.transfer/transferFrom calls fail with tokens that don't return bool (ABI decode revert) or return false without reverting (silent failure enabling free locking)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats stem from the same missing SafeERC20 usage. TECH-H014 selected for HIGH rating and coverage of both failure modes (ABI decode revert for missing return AND silent failure for false return). Note: TECH2-H004 here is 'Free Token Locking via Unchecked ERC20 Return Values' (the first entry with that ID).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Zero lock duration enables instant lock/unlock when both playerSettings.lockDuration and lockdrop.minLockDuration are 0, allowing reward gaming without time commitment
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 6 threats describe the same zero-duration lock exploitation path. ECON2-H011 specifically highlights the configureLockdrop validation gap but shares the same root cause (no minimum duration enforcement). ECON-H004 selected for HIGH rating with comprehensive attack economics including flash loan consideration.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: proposeUSDPrice applies a single price to all listed token contracts, enabling systematic mispricing when tokens with different USD values are bundled
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 5 threats describe the same design flaw: _execUSDPriceUpdate loops over contracts applying a single proposedPrice. ECON2-H006 selected for HIGH rating with strongest economic impact analysis (thousands of times reward inflation scenario).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: proposeUSDPrice auto-approval does not call _execUSDPriceUpdate, so APPROVE_THRESHOLD=1 effectively requires 2 approvals, breaking governance semantics
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 4 threats describe the same logic defect: _execUSDPriceUpdate is only called in approveUSDPrice, not proposeUSDPrice. TECH-H017 selected for clearest technical description. Note: TECH2-H005 here is 'Price governance deadlock risk: threshold set to 1' (the second entry with that ID).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ETH unlock uses payable(msg.sender).transfer() with 2300 gas stipend, permanently freezing ETH for smart contract wallet users whose receive/fallback needs more gas
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 7 threats describe the same .transfer() 2300 gas limitation causing permanent ETH freeze for contract wallets. ECON2-H009 selected for HIGH rating with comprehensive victim impact analysis and explicit note about missing alternative withdrawal mechanism. Note: TECH2-H004 here is 'ETH unlock uses .transfer(2300 gas)' and TECH2-H009 is 'ETH Funds Frozen for Smart Contract Wallets' (both from different specialist runs).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: getLockedWeightedValue reverts or overflows: underflow from 10^(18-decimals) when decimals>18, and potential overflow from deltaDecimal*quantity*usdPrice before division
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 3 threats target mathematical issues in getLockedWeightedValue. TECH-H018 covers both the decimals>18 underflow and the large-value overflow, while the others each cover one. Note: TECH2-H006 here is 'Denial of Service in getLockedWeightedValue for Tokens with >18 Decimals' (first entry) and TECH2-H008 is 'Overflow/revert risk in getLockedWeightedValue' (second entry).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded configuredTokenContracts array grows without limit or removal, causing gas exhaustion DoS in setLockDuration, getLockedWeightedValue, and getLocked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same unbounded array iteration DoS. OPS2-H008 selected for HIGH rating and broader coverage of affected functions.
